title: Configuring tzdata failed when running ubuntu container
body:
### Issue Description

I set the `tz = "local"` in containers.conf file, I am using WSL2 Ubuntu.

When I try to configure tzdata I got following error, this seems the `/etc/localtime` is mounted into containers from host.

```
Configuring tzdata
------------------

Please select the geographic area in which you live. Subsequent configuration
questions will narrow this down by presenting a list of cities, representing
the time zones in which they are located.

  1. Africa      4. Arctic    7. Australia  10. Pacific
  2. America     5. Asia      8. Europe     11. Etc
  3. Antarctica  6. Atlantic  9. Indian     12. Legacy
Geographic area:
Use of uninitialized value $_[1] in join or string at /usr/share/perl5/Debconf/DbDriver/Stack.pm line 112.
mv: cannot move '/etc/localtime.dpkg-new' to '/etc/localtime': Device or resource busy
Use of uninitialized value $val in substitution (s///) at /usr/share/perl5/Debconf/Format/822.pm line 84, <GEN6> line 4.
Use of uninitialized value $val in concatenation (.) or string at /usr/share/perl5/Debconf/Format/822.pm line 85, <GEN6> line 4.
dpkg: error processing package tzdata (--configure):
 installed tzdata package post-installation script subprocess returned error exit status 1
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

```
podman run -it --rm ubuntu:24.04

apt update
apt -y install tzdata
```

### Describe the results you received

configure tzdata failed

### Describe the results you expected

should no error

### podman info output

```yaml
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.0.2
API Version:  5.0.2
Go Version:   go1.22.2
Git Commit:   3304dd95b8978a8346b96b7d43134990609b3b29
Built:        Sat Apr 20 17:05:23 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.5.0
API Version:  5.5.0
Go Version:   go1.24.3
Git Commit:   0dbcb51477ee7ab8d3b47d30facf71fc38bb0c98
Built:        Sat May 24 19:17:46 2025
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
I am not able to reproduce this when using the default podman machine (i.e. `podman machine init --now` from powershell).

@chenzhiwei please share how we can setup the WSL instance and install Podman to reproduce your problem.

windows or WSL do not matter. /etc/localtime is a bind mount from the host and since it is a mount point it cannot be moved thus the error.

tz has actually two different behaviours depending on if tzdata exists in the container or not https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/34c258b419ea359d2d4c3dd70a1491db9a256d06.
If it does exist we create a symlink to the proper timezone file in the container in which case dpkg should be fine to reconfigure the tz.

Logically I think the behavior make sense. If you want to manage the timezone from within the container don't make podman manage it. I don't see what we could change to allow this to work.

I build podman and its dependencies from source code inside WSL2 Ubuntu.

Did you change your `containers.conf` file to set the `tz = "local"`?

@Luap99 The thing is I don't know which package depends on tzdata, I am trying to install some packages inside a container. 

Building container images will also encounter such error if there is a package depends on tzdata.

Well you do control if you set `tz=local` so don't use it if you have containers that must configure their own timezone like that.

I know that this is not a nice thing to do but really there is no good way to avoid that. The only thing podman could do is to not bind mount the file but then this means we would need to write a copy of the file directly into the container which is not something we want to generally.

Normally one would set `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` to avoid the configure prompt from the package but doing this it seem it still tries to replace /etc/localtime. Maybe it would be good to file a bug against the tzdata package and ask to ignore such errors? Or ask ubuntu to include tzdata in the base image? (It seems the debian image has tzdata in the base so it is not a problem there)
